item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Sad that people didn't like the AWCY reference since that's what the article screamed at me.

item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded: The second image is used with permission, as indicated here. However, the first image has been removed.

item.title: Provenance
item.content:encoded:
the top pic is, apparently, Disneyland in the 50s, before they standardized the look of the characters. I've traced it as far back as here - https://imgur.com/swPsikK - notice that it was uploaded to imgur in July 2013, two months before this article was written. Imgur is obviously not its ultimate provenance, but beyond that, I have no idea. Assuming it's real and not an edit, there's no indication that it's suitably licensed. And even if it is an edit, there's no indication that the source image is suitably licensed. (Tapiture apparently had it in May 2013, but they're dead and archive.org didn't keep every single page.)

Image #2, I have no idea. Obviously a statue in some gallery somewhere.

item.title: Staff Post - Wikiwalk Proposal
item.content:encoded:
Timer Expired. Edits made as discussed below. Author may revert at any time.

The WikiWalk team is proposing edits to this page. All proposed edits will be in a collapsible below this post, and after 7 days if there are no serious objections to the edits, the feedback received will be taken into account as the edits are made. If you have questions not directly related to the article about this project, please pm a member of the WikiWalk Team

+ Show Block
- Hide Block

It was pointed out in a conversation with fellow wikiwalker stormbreath that SCP-040's anomalous ability to reshape matter and that which is seen in this skip here are quite similar, and that it would not be out of the realm of possibility for the SCPF to take a look for a possible connection. As such, I am recommending the following edits:

Changing this:

SCP-1490 will then proceed to shape SCP-1490-2 to resemble organisms from the kingdom Animalia (recently logged creations include a tiger, a frog, and an octopus). Methods utilized by SCP-1490 include repeatedly slamming its head onto SCP-1490-2, kicking or striking with its limbs to create depressions, biting and pulling at SCP-1490-2 to form "limbs" or to tear off unneeded material, and muttering into SCP-1490-2's ear.

To this:

SCP-1490 will then proceed to shape SCP-1490-2 to resemble organisms from the kingdom Animalia (recently logged creations include a tiger, a frog, and an octopus). Methods utilized by SCP-1490 include repeatedly slamming its head onto SCP-1490-2, kicking or striking with its limbs to create depressions, biting and pulling at SCP-1490-2 to form "limbs" or to tear off unneeded material, and muttering into SCP-1490-2's ear.1

If anyone has other/better examples that could be used here please leave a comment saying so!

Footnotes
1. Similarities between this ability to reshape biological matter and that observed in SCP-040 has been noted. Investigation into a potential shared source of the two anomalies is ongoing.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like it

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Looks alright to me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Just noting that I made a minor grammar fix. Staff or author, feel free to do the revert thing if I did a stupid.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
and muttering into SCP-1490-2's ear.

hahahaha, what the fuck jesus christ

The ending note was ehh in some ways, but the rest of the article more than made up for it. I do like the assumption that the kid only pretends to be happy and jovial in order to sculpt people, when really, he's a little shit.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I fixed a minor grammar error, I hope no one minds

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I want my work to [illegible]

"be cool"?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I went from 'Awh…' during the containment procedures very quickly to 'yeeerk!' Good job, this one is nasty.

item.title: Author Post
item.content:encoded:
Since others are doing this, I might as well do it too.

Meserach found the first image, so kudos to him.

Reddit user mcrfreak78 posted the second image, which can be found here. Messaged them about it and they were delighted, so that's nice. :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
By popular demand, I removed the reference to AWCY. Sorry to those who liked it.

See the last post on the previous page by Abbetik; I edited it to be more like that.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: If the others are feeling like I was, maybe it's just a little heavy-handed. Maybe if you replace the word 'cool' for something else, like I dunno, 'accepted' or 'understood' or 'complete,' but you leave the subtle reference to a mysterious group who approached him, it would work a lot better. Then, people who like the reference can still 'find' it but it's not forced.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This seems to me like some kind of twisted Disney princess, with "animal" friends and all. Also, lose the AWCY? reference.

No vote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Nah, I'm sure that the AWCY? being taken out won't effect the overall article, and I'm sure that it isn't the reason people upvoted. Personally, I'd just take it out and see what the reaction is. All in all, though, good show.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
My only question is, why are they -2 and not -1?

I think it's more of a matter of personal preference in writing.
I really want to do something about the AWCY? , but I can't tell if that's the part many other people like.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
muttering into SCP-1490-2's ear.

What in the serious fuck. That's maybe the most disturbing part of this whole article.

I agree with Deacon Blues above about the AWCY reference. My only question is, why are they -2 and not -1?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Right about to upvoted, and then AWCY? out of nowhere. I like AWCY?, but I also like them to be used where it makes sense.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Enh. I find the AWCY? reference kind of annoying, and the first pic totally doesn't go with the rest of the article, in my opinion, because it's so clearly of early Minnie & Mickey Mouse costumes.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's brother bear? I didn't realize that until you said. D:

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I actually think it is subtle enough. I liked the fact at a) it doesn't have an AWCY tag, and b) apparently he's not cool yet.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'll change it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I liked it…imo this is AWCY done right. It's not ostentatious about it, but rather subtle. I wasn't expecting them to pop up at the end. And creepy AF on the kid's sculpting methods. +1

E: Well, scratch the AWCY reference. Good article either way. Highly enjoyed it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
You know, I was kinda feeling sorry for the kid in the beginning, he just wanted his mom and dad. Then I read on, and I was like "You….Little….Bastard.
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You need to lose the capital O on "Osteoporosis". I think?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Man, I loved this when you posted the early draft in chat, and the improvements since then have made it much better, especially that second creepy as hell picture. Was nearly willing to upvote as it was, now you have my +1 for the combination of creepy kid and body horror in a unique and shudder-worthy way.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: "When I grow up, I wanna be an author like you!" -Semi-quote of my niece. Replace "author" with "chef". I am not even a chef.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I posted it to Visual Records after it showed up on a forum I frequent. No idea where it ultimately came from, though. You know, other than nightmarish alternate Disneyland.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Definitely. The big-headed kid was cute, but I'm glad you think these are better.
I think Meserach found the first one, so kudos to him.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: These pictures are much better than the one with the big headed guy.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I was trying to go for a more subtler AWCY reference, but I guess if enough people don't like it I may rewrite it.

item.title: No, SCP-1490 doesn't get to hang out with the cool kids
item.content:encoded: Add me to the people who find the AWCY? reference tacked on and unnecessary.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thanks! I'll try to work on the wording of the transformation process as soon as I can, though I'd just give the excuse that the boy had gone off the deep end by the time he wrote that last note, but I understand what you mean.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
That AWCY? reference actively ruins it for me. In addition, there's some awkward language (the muscles of SCP-1490-2 etc.) and the final addendum is incredibly forced. It feels awfully convenient that he wrote the clue to the article for us to find.

Anyway, I like the way you've portrayed his sculpting techniques and the pics are gold too, especially the first one. Congratulations on your third successful scip, Accelerando. :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thanks! Working on it now, with the slightest influence from CryogenChaos.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Nice work. Now go make an author page!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: +1 I've seen the pictures posted here before but this is well done. I'm glad to see more AWCY scips, and it's done in a subtle way.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Ah, I see! Thanks for the info.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I fixed the sapience thing. :)

+ Collapsible cus spoiler
- Press to hide

I would get rid of the word sapience and also change the word "cool" at the end

Ok, I know that you're generally new still, and haven't heard much of he Groups if Interest. I also know you stated that an SCP should be independent and not rely on anything else. The usage of the word "cool" is a reference towards the art group "Are We Cool Yet?", which is basically a group of art terrorists.

If you don't like that part, I apologize. I'm glad the "sculpting" process is where the article is the most interesting part for you; the addendum was meant to catch other people off guard.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
What. The. Shit. +1

You had me when you explained how he 'sculpts' them. I mean… just what the shit.

I would get rid of the word sapience and also change the word "cool" at the end. It sort of throws the whole mood/tone of the article off. Unless you think it's absolutely necessary, I would change it to "happy" or "complete" or "loved" or something.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'll try to address that it displays no sapience in the presence of personnel. It is sort of a traumatized child.

Edit: On second thought, I'll just mention it reacts only to those affected by it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: First of all, I sweared at the second I saw the pictures (Holy shit they are so wrong). Now, I fell rather disapointed. I expected a lot more from the article, but the hook wasn't as good as the pics. Also, you say it displays no sapience, but he can write letters? I dont think that's possible. -1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Edit: And fixed.
And fixed by Aelanna. Sorry.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Accelerando, you may want to localize the files. The links could go dead, and then what're you gonna do?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That's the brother, by the way. Thanks. :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Liked this when you showed it in chat, and now you've added another creepy picture on it.
Have a +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: …GDI Azzle, I didn't even notice that until you pointed it out. :<

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Would be better without AWCY? reference.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Special thanks to all of the people in chat. Also, Dexanote (For keeping me from copying another SCP) Roget (For preventing me from going with a vomiting kid as an SCP), and FlameShirt (Picture formatting is irritating).

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Damn it. I feex now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Boy, fix them SCP-XXXXs.
